lede: A look at April 7 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Rafaela Gómez and Jakub Smrž, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Ketanji Brown Jackson
events.desc: Ketanji Brown Jackson is confirmed for the Supreme Court of the United States, becoming the first black female justice.

events.title: 2017 Stockholm truck attack
events.desc: A man deliberately drives a hijacked truck into a crowd of people in Stockholm, Sweden, killing five people and injuring fifteen others.

events.title: NASA
events.desc: NASA launches the 2001 Mars Odyssey orbiter.

events.title: First Chechen War
events.desc: First Chechen War: Russian paramilitary troops begin a massacre of civilians in Samashki, Chechnya.

events.title: Viet Cong
events.desc: Vietnam War: Communist forces overrun the South Vietnamese town of Loc Ninh.

events.title: Francoist Spain
events.desc: Francoist Spain agrees to surrender its protectorate in Morocco.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Imperial Japanese Navy battleship Yamato, one of the two largest ever constructed, is sunk by United States Navy aircraft during Operation Ten-Go.

events.title: Benito Mussolini
events.desc: Benito Mussolini declares an Italian protectorate over Albania and forces King Zog I into exile.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: The Union's Army of the Tennessee and the Army of the Ohio defeat the Confederate Army of Mississippi near Shiloh, Tennessee.

events.title: Pedro II of Brazil
events.desc: Pedro II becomes emperor of the Empire of Brazil.

deaths.text: Mircea Lucescu, Romanian football player and manager
Josep Maria Subirachs, Spanish sculptor and painter
Frank Church, American soldier, lawyer, and politician

observances.text: Christian feast days: Blessed Edward Oldcorne and Blessed Ralph Ashley
Christian feast days: Jean-Baptiste de La Salle
